title: How hot is the hottest chocolate in the world?
content_raw:
```html

The "hottest chocolate in the world" can vary depending on the specific brand or recipe being used, but one of the hottest known chocolates is the "Carolina Reaper" chocolate, made by the company "Mad Dog 357". The Carolina Reaper is currently the world's hottest chili pepper, with an average Scoville rating of over 1.5 million units. Mad Dog 357's Carolina Reaper chocolate has a Scoville rating of 9 million units, making it extremely spicy. To put this in perspective, a jalapeño pepper typically has a Scoville rating of around 2,500 to 8,000 units, while a habanero pepper can range from 100,000 to 350,000 units. The Carolina Reaper is significantly hotter than both of these peppers, and the Carolina Reaper chocolate is even hotter.

Consuming very spicy foods, such as the Carolina Reaper chocolate, can cause discomfort or even physical reactions in some individuals, such as heartburn, sweating, or difficulty breathing. It's important to approach these types of products with caution and only consume them if you are comfortable with the level of spiciness involved. Additionally, individuals with pre-existing medical conditions or sensitivities to spicy foods should consult with a doctor before consuming these types of products.

If you're looking to explore extreme heat experiences with friends, Carolina Reaper chocolate can be an exciting addition to lolly buffet ideas or party lolly selections, though it's certainly not for the faint of heart. For those seeking similarly intense flavour experiences, there are other spicy options like Takis Australia or Flamin Hot Cheetos, which offer serious heat but at slightly more approachable levels.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can you buy Mad Dog 357 Carolina Reaper chocolate in Australia?

Mad Dog 357 Carolina Reaper chocolate is available through specialty online retailers in Australia, though it may not be stocked in regular supermarkets due to its extreme heat level. We recommend checking with specialist hot sauce and extreme food suppliers, as availability can vary by state and retailer.

You may also want to buy

  • Jelly Joy Stick 20g- Sweetsworld - Chocolate Shop$0.5
  • Lil Nitro Hot Gummy Bear 3g -The World's Hottest Gummy Bear$19.99
  • WORLD'S HOTTEST CORN CHIPS 50G$19.99

What should I do if I accidentally eat too much Carolina Reaper chocolate?

If you experience discomfort, drink milk or eat dairy products like yogurt or ice cream, as capsaicin (the compound that makes peppers hot) is fat-soluble and milk helps neutralize the heat. Avoid water, as it won't help, and seek medical attention if you experience difficulty breathing or severe symptoms.

Is Carolina Reaper chocolate actually chocolate, or just a novelty product?

Yes, Mad Dog 357's Carolina Reaper chocolate is real chocolate infused with Carolina Reaper pepper extract, making it a legitimate (though extremely spicy) chocolate product. It's designed more as a challenge or extreme food experience rather than a casual treat, so it's not recommended for everyday eating.

How does 9 million Scoville units compare to other hot foods I might know?

To put it in perspective, Carolina Reaper chocolate at 9 million Scoville units is roughly 1,125 times hotter than a jalapeño pepper and about 26 times hotter than a habanero. It's comparable to pure capsaicin extract and is far beyond anything found in mainstream spicy foods.

Are there any age restrictions on buying extremely hot chocolate like this in Australia?

While there are no strict legal age restrictions for purchasing extremely spicy foods in Australia, most retailers recommend these products for adults only.
```
